title: Help the community switching to the new Windows installer
body:
### Feature request description

Podman Desktop, [winget](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs/tree/master/manifests/r/RedHat/Podman), [chocolatey](https://github.com/lareeth/choco-podman-cli) and [scoop](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Main/blob/master/bucket/podman.json) are using the legacy windows installer. We should assist them migrating to the new installer to avoid breakage when the 6.0 is released.

**We may want to wait for a 5.7.1 to ask to update the installers**. Podman 5.7.0 is affected by [a bug](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27603) that makes Hyper-V `machine init` fail if Podman has been installed with the new installer.

### Suggest potential solution

We should open issues or PRs such as those projects update the name of the installer that they pull from our GitHub releases.

### Additional context

- https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/15067
- https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs/issues/360516
- https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Main/pull/8070
- https://github.com/lareeth/choco-podman-cli/pull/54

comments.body:
you will also have to move podman-machine-os CI
https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/main/contrib/cirrus/windows_setup.ps1

> We may want to wait for a 5.7.1 to ask to update the installers. Podman 5.7.0 is affected by https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27603 that makes Hyper-V machine init fail if Podman has been installed with the new installer.

We branched everywhere so this won't included in a 5.7.1 on its own unless we started branching container-libs backport it there than make a new release and then vendor that into podman 5.7. Assuming you talk about https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/496?

> We branched everywhere so this won't included in a 5.7.1 on its own unless we started branching container-libs backport it there than make a new release and then vendor that into podman 5.7. Assuming you talk about [containers/container-libs#496](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/496)?

Yes, I am talking about that. An alternative, to avoid creating the branch in container-lib and do the vendoring in the 5.7 branch, would be to set the `helper_binaries_dir` in the config file generated by the installer (in the 5.7 branch only). I would prefer to vendor the container-lib but if that present some risk we have that option too.

I will add the subtask to update https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/main/contrib/cirrus/windows_setup.ps1 in this [issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27624) that is the "internal" one.

> Yes, I am talking about that. An alternative, to avoid creating the branch in container-lib and do the vendoring in the 5.7 branch, would be to set the helper_binaries_dir in the config file generated by the installer (in the 5.7 branch only). I would prefer to vendor the container-lib but if that present some risk we have that option too.

Yes we should do the vendor, just pointing out that needs some work that must happen by someone.
I am sure we find a few other bug fixes in container-libs that might be worth to backport as well so no big deal overall.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as done. The chocolatey package isn't updated yet. But a PR is open, and the chocolatey package wasn't using the legacy installer anyway.
